title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2026
summary: The Bank of Canada released its January 2026 Monetary Policy Report, maintaining the overnight policy rate at 2.25%. The report highlights that US tariffs continue to disrupt the Canadian economy, with growth expected to remain modest and inflation staying close to the 2% target. The outlook for Canada's trade agreement with the United States and Mexico remains highly uncertain, contributing to elevated geopolitical risks.
key_points: Bank of Canada holds target overnight rate at 2.25% (Bank Rate at 2.5%, deposit rate at 2.20%)
CPI inflation remains near the 2% target over the projection horizon
Economic growth expected to average around 1.25% over the projection horizon
US tariffs remain elevated and continue to disrupt Canadian economy with structural adjustments underway
Canada-US-Mexico Agreement review outcome remains highly uncertain with wide-ranging potential impacts
Global growth supported by AI investment boom and fiscal stimulus in major economies
why_it_matters: The Bank of Canada's decision to hold rates steady signals a cautious approach amid tariff-related economic disruption. The modest growth outlook combined with stable inflation near target suggests the central bank is navigating a challenging environment where trade uncertainty weighs on growth while inflation remains contained. The uncertainty around the North American trade agreement creates significant forward-looking risk for Canadian economic planning.
macro_context: Canada faces elevated geopolitical uncertainty with its major trade agreement under review. US tariff disruptions are causing structural adjustments in the Canadian economy, with businesses adapting by seeking non-US export markets and reducing reliance on US imports. Excess supply roughly offsets upward cost pressures, keeping inflation near the 2% target.
